scope: LP-06 prompt caching support: Anthropic cache-control breakpoints, OpenAI prompt-cache-key continuity, cached-token extraction, and cached-input cost accounting.

requiredBehaviors: Strategic messages are marked with cacheControl: system prefix, last non-tool user message, and last tool result that survived normalization.
Anthropic request shaping serializes those markers as cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" } on the final content block for each marked message.
A request carries no more than three automatically selected cache_control breakpoints from the normal message path.
Cache-control markers can appear on system text, user text or image-following text, and tool_result blocks.
edgeCases: Orphan tool results are dropped before cache-control selection.
Image URL content preserves the image block and puts cache_control on the final text block.
Tool results with mixed content preserve nested text/image content while attaching cache_control to the tool_result block.
commands: cd runtime && npx vitest run src/llm/wire/messages-anthropic.test.ts

requiredBehaviors: Responses API requests carry a stable prompt_cache_key derived from the caller option or conversation id.
OpenAI Responses usage reads input_tokens_details.cached_tokens into cachedInputTokens.
OpenAI Chat Completions usage reads prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens into cachedInputTokens.
Cached-input usage is preserved through the provider adapter into session token_count events.
edgeCases: Continuation requests preserve previous_response_id while keeping prompt_cache_key stable.
Responses streaming completion with web search and function calls still records cachedInputTokens.
Chat Completions streaming completion still records cachedInputTokens alongside reasoning tokens.
commands: cd runtime && npx vitest run src/llm/wire/responses-openai.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai/adapter.test.ts

rows.source: official OpenAI API pricing and prompt-caching references checked on 2026-05-02
requiredBehaviors: OpenAI-hosted gpt-4o cached input is priced at 0.00125 USD per 1K cached input tokens.
OpenAI-hosted gpt-4o-mini cached input is priced at 0.000075 USD per 1K cached input tokens.
OpenAI-hosted GPT-5.4, GPT-5, GPT-4.1, o1, o3, and o4-mini families have explicit cached-input tiers instead of collapsing into a broader fallback tier.
OpenAI cached input is treated as a subset of reported input tokens and is not double-charged at the full input rate.
Provider-scoped local runtimes such as lmstudio resolve before bare OpenAI model names, so local cached tokens remain zero-cost.
Distinct GPT-5 subfamilies keep distinct pricing; newer GPT-5.4 models do not collapse into the older GPT-5 tier.
edgeCases: A model string of gpt-4o with provider openai resolves to openai:gpt-4o.
A model string of gpt-4o-mini with provider openai resolves to openai:gpt-4o-mini.
Model strings gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-4.1, and o4-mini each resolve to their own provider-aware pricing entries.
A full-cache OpenAI usage payload with inputTokens equal to cachedInputTokens charges only the cached-input rate.
A partial-cache OpenAI usage payload charges fresh input at the full rate and cached input at the cached rate.
A local provider reporting model gpt-4o does not fall through to the bare gpt-4o cached-input tier.
commands: cd runtime && npx vitest run src/session/cost.test.ts
